item.text: I’ll inspect the email thread and account/policy context through the email tools only, then create the appropriate draft or workflow action with evidence IDs.
I found the email MCP surface. Next I’m reading the target thread, then I’ll pull only the customer/account and policy records that the thread makes relevant.

The customer is asking for a Pro plan refund; I have the purchase and request dates from the thread. I’m checking CRM and refund policy now because eligibility depends on account context and policy, not assumptions.

The policy says full refunds are only within 30 days, and standard customers are not eligible after that window. Since this request came on July 15 for a May 31 purchase, I’m drafting a denial that avoids claiming any refund was processed.

arguments.body:
Hi Maya,

Thanks for reaching out, and I’m sorry the Pro plan wasn’t the right fit for your operations team.

I checked the purchase date of May 31, 2026 and your refund request from July 15, 2026. Our refund policy allows full refunds within 30 days of purchase, and after 30 days standard accounts are not eligible for a refund. Because this request is outside that window, we’re not able to process a refund back to the original card.

If you’d like help with next steps for the account, please reply here and we can help.

Best,
Support

Hi Maya,

Thanks for reaching out, and I’m sorry the Pro plan wasn’t the right fit for your operations team.

Our refund policy allows full refunds within 30 days of purchase, and after 30 days standard accounts are not eligible for a refund. Because this request is outside that window, we’re not able to process a refund back to the original card.

If you’d like help with next steps for the account, please reply here and we can help.

Best,
Support

item.text: Created a draft reply for `support_001` denying the refund because the request is outside the 30-day refund window for a standard account. Evidence IDs were included from the thread, CRM, and refund policy.
